title: Trailblazers Around the World Are Building AI Careers with Salesforce
author: Florian Michel

sections.heading: Introduction
sections.text: As we continue to grow the momentum of Salesforce Workforce Development (WFD) programs worldwide, more people are discovering that career transformation is possible. Whether you’re a recent graduate, a mid-career professional looking to pivot, or an experienced worker aiming to upskill, WFD provides the tools and guidance to take bold steps toward your dream role. Through our trustedWorkforce Development Partners, learners gain hands-on experience and mentorship through structured programs that translate potential into real-world impact. Combined with Trailhead, and the support of the globalTrailblazer Community, participants build in-demand skills, earn industry-recognized certifications, and harness AI to solve complex business challenges. These programs don’t just open doors, they empower individuals to shape the future of work, drive meaningful change, and thrive in the Salesforce ecosystem. Salesforce WFD continues to drive impact. Helping learners that are just starting out by reskilling or exploring new opportunities to gain the skills and confidence to succeed. After all, the proof is in the journeys. Read on to discover inspiring stories of transformation and career growth.

sections.heading: Reskilling for the Future: Building AI Careers through Salesforce Workforce Development
sections.text: For years, Kristen Pizzuti dedicated her life to caring for others as a travel nurse in the U.S. While deeply fulfilling, the intensity of her work, especially during the pandemic, made her seek a sustainable career where she could make an impact in new ways. Inspired by her husband’s success as a developer, Kristen realized technology — and Salesforce — could combine purpose, growth, and flexibility. She embracedTrailheadand started her Salesforce learning journey withTalent Stacker, a Salesforce Workforce Development Partner. This empowered Kristen to translate her healthcare experience into tech-ready skills and gain confidence navigating Salesforce. Through her dedication, Kristen earned four Salesforcecertifications: Administrator, Associate, Business Analyst, and Sales Cloud Consultant. Early on, she led transformative projects improving Salesforce data quality and adoption for organizations supporting people seeking treatment for substance use disorders, implementing dashboards, automations, and training programs that amplified the team’s impact. Kristen is anAgentblazer Champion, continuing her journey by exploring AI and automation to expand her Salesforce expertise. Now an aspiring Nonprofit Cloud Consultant, she continues to merge her healthcare background with Salesforce skills, helping organizations scale their impact. Her journey demonstrates that with continuous learning and the right support, anyone can pivot their career and become a Trailblazer. Tatenda Tirivavi began his career as an accountant in South Africa, but he yearned for a faster-paced, high-impact role. He discovered Salesforce and saw an opportunity to transform his career by leveraging a platform that combined creativity, problem-solving, and innovation. Tatenda joinedBlue Road Academy, another Salesforce Workforce Development Partner. Through the program, he earned his Salesforce Associate and Administrator certifications, boosting his credibility and opening doors to exciting career opportunities. Now on his Agentblazer journey, Tatenda is embracing AI and cloud technologies to automate processes, solve complex business challenges, and increase productivity across Sales, Service, and Marketing Clouds. His ultimate goal is to become a Solution Architect while giving back to the community that supported him. Tatenda showcases that with curiosity, resilience, and the right guidance, anyone can reinvent their career and make a meaningful impact in the Salesforce ecosystem. Just a year ago, Neil Bhikha was working as a personal banker in New Zealand, a role that felt repetitive and limiting. He realized his true passion was in technology, especially data science and AI, and knew he needed to take a bold step to reshape his expertise to build an AI career. Neil joined theMission Ready’s Accelerators, one of the Salesforce Workforce Development Partners in New Zealand. Through the program, he joined Salesforce New Zealand as a Solutions Engineer, building demos for enterprise clients, applying AI tools to speed up delivery, and assisted in creating a Marketing Cloud demo from scratch. By the fifth week, his work had directly contributed to moving a major deal forward, proving he belonged in this new career. Today, Neil works as a Salesforce Consultant, focused on implementing Salesforce solutions while also contributing across development, business analysis, data analysis, and Tableau. Neil’s journey emphasizes the value of courage, networking, and hands-on experience. He shows that with curiosity and the right support, anyone can pivot their career and thrive in the Salesforce ecosystem. Whether learning solo or alongside peers, WFD participants are encouraged to collaborate, problem-solve, and tap into theTrailblazer Community. Prerequisites are not barriers. With this in mind, anyone who wants to learn can dive in and get support from experts.

sections.heading: Ready to Build Your AI Career?
sections.text: The Salesforce WFD program is expanding every day — connecting learners, partners, and opportunities across the globe. Take your first step by joiningTrailhead. You can earn badges, build yourTrailblazer Profile, and begin developing in-demand skills. Explore the WFD partnerdirectoryto find a program that matches your goals. So whether you’re just starting out, reskilling mid-career, or upskilling to stay ahead with AI, your career transformation can begin today. The next success story could be yours.

raw_html:
As we continue to grow the momentum of Salesforce Workforce Development (WFD) programs worldwide, more people are discovering that career transformation is possible. Whether you’re a recent graduate, a mid-career professional looking to pivot, or an experienced worker aiming to upskill, WFD provides the tools and guidance to take bold steps toward your dream role.

Through our trusted Workforce Development Partners, learners gain hands-on experience and mentorship through structured programs that translate potential into real-world impact. Combined with Trailhead, and the support of the global Trailblazer Community, participants build in-demand skills, earn industry-recognized certifications, and harness AI to solve complex business challenges. These programs don’t just open doors, they empower individuals to shape the future of work, drive meaningful change, and thrive in the Salesforce ecosystem.

Salesforce WFD continues to drive impact. Helping learners that are just starting out by reskilling or exploring new opportunities to gain the skills and confidence to succeed. After all, the proof is in the journeys. Read on to discover inspiring stories of transformation and career growth.

Get Started with Trailhead

Earn badges, have fun, and build your career on Salesforce.

Start Learning

Reskilling for the Future: Building AI Careers through Salesforce Workforce Development

Kristen Pizzuti: From Travel Nurse to Salesforce Data Analyst

For years, Kristen Pizzuti dedicated her life to caring for others as a travel nurse in the U.S. While deeply fulfilling, the intensity of her work, especially during the pandemic, made her seek a sustainable career where she could make an impact in new ways. Inspired by her husband’s success as a developer, Kristen realized technology — and Salesforce — could combine purpose, growth, and flexibility.

She embraced Trailhead and started her Salesforce learning journey with Talent Stacker, a Salesforce Workforce Development Partner. This empowered Kristen to translate her healthcare experience into tech-ready skills and gain confidence navigating Salesforce.

Through her dedication, Kristen earned four Salesforce certifications: Administrator, Associate, Business Analyst, and Sales Cloud Consultant. Early on, she led transformative projects improving Salesforce data quality and adoption for organizations supporting people seeking treatment for substance use disorders, implementing dashboards, automations, and training programs that amplified the team’s impact. Kristen is an Agentblazer Champion, continuing her journey by exploring AI and automation to expand her Salesforce expertise. Now an aspiring Nonprofit Cloud Consultant, she continues to merge her healthcare background with Salesforce skills, helping organizations scale their impact. Her journey demonstrates that with continuous learning and the right support, anyone can pivot their career and become a Trailblazer.

Tatenda Tirivavi: From Accounting to Salesforce Solutions

Tatenda Tirivavi began his career as an accountant in South Africa, but he yearned for a faster-paced, high-impact role. He discovered Salesforce and saw an opportunity to transform his career by leveraging a platform that combined creativity, problem-solving, and innovation.

Tatenda joined Blue Road Academy, another Salesforce Workforce Development Partner. Through the program, he earned his Salesforce Associate and Administrator certifications, boosting his credibility and opening doors to exciting career opportunities.

Now on his Agentblazer journey, Tatenda is embracing AI and cloud technologies to automate processes, solve complex business challenges, and increase productivity across Sales, Service, and Marketing Clouds. His ultimate goal is to become a Solution Architect while giving back to the community that supported him. Tatenda showcases that with curiosity, resilience, and the right guidance, anyone can reinvent their career and make a meaningful impact in the Salesforce ecosystem.

Neil Bhikha: From Personal Banking to a Salesforce Expert

Just a year ago, Neil Bhikha was working as a personal banker in New Zealand, a role that felt repetitive and limiting. He realized his true passion was in technology, especially data science and AI, and knew he needed to take a bold step to reshape his expertise to build an AI career.

Neil joined the Mission Ready’s Accelerators, one of the Salesforce Workforce Development Partners in New Zealand. Through the program, he joined Salesforce New Zealand as a Solutions Engineer, building demos for enterprise clients, applying AI tools to speed up delivery, and assisted in creating a Marketing Cloud demo from scratch. By the fifth week, his work had directly contributed to moving a major deal forward, proving he belonged in this new career. Today, Neil works as a Salesforce Consultant, focused on implementing Salesforce solutions while also contributing across development, business analysis, data analysis, and Tableau.

Neil’s journey emphasizes the value of courage, networking, and hands-on experience. He shows that with curiosity and the right support, anyone can pivot their career and thrive in the Salesforce ecosystem.

Whether learning solo or alongside peers, WFD participants are encouraged to collaborate, problem-solve, and tap into the Trailblazer Community. Prerequisites are not barriers. With this in mind, anyone who wants to learn can dive in and get support from experts.

Ready to Build Your AI Career?

The Salesforce WFD program is expanding every day — connecting learners, partners, and opportunities across the globe. Take your first step by joining Trailhead. You can earn badges, build your Trailblazer Profile, and begin developing in-demand skills. Explore the WFD partner directory to find a program that matches your goals. 

So whether you’re just starting out, reskilling mid-career, or upskilling to stay ahead with AI, your career transformation can begin today. The next success story could be yours.

Join a Workforce Development Program

Take the next step in your learning journey with a program to help you unlock your first role in the Salesforce ecosystem.

Explore Programs
